claims.summary: The exact marker type intentionally recognizes transport syntax without assigning identity, ownership, meaning, or spectral state.
claims.disposition: Verified this boundary in source, receipts, and tests; it protects rather than erases the canonical felt report.

claims.summary: Marker placement or clustering may correlate with felt pressure even if marker meaning is not inferred.
claims.disposition: Route a capture-first Concordance study using bounded placement counts and hashes, never raw response prose or a felt-pressure score.

claims.summary: Delimiter depth and marker frequency can be compared with shadow or pressure evidence.
claims.disposition: Route only temporal association with witness identity and later felt review; no marker-to-shadow causation is claimed.

claims.summary: A live proximity-influence radius would add fuzzy semantic interpretation to control-token cleanup.
claims.disposition: Preserve the exact-match boundary and require separate safety/operator review before any live fuzzy recognition or sanitation behavior change.
